Edge Case: Sole trader registered under personal name (no business name)
Our Response: Two paths depending on ABR result: (1) If ABR result has a registered trading name (e.g. "Ferguson Fencing"), _has_registered_trading_name is true — goes straight to enrichment using the trading name for Brave/Google and legal name (FERGUSON, MARTIN) for licence search. (2) If no trading name registered, _is_sole_trader_personal_name() detects SURNAME,FIRSTNAME format or Individual entity type. Shows card with ABN/location, asks "What name does your business trade under?" User provides their trading name, which becomes display_name for Brave/Google searches; legal name stays as licence search term. In both paths, all enrichment results (Google Places, Brave web, licence) feed into a DATA CONFIDENCE assessment. When results are sparse or clearly wrong (e.g. politician Wikipedia results for "Martin Ferguson"), AI sees low confidence signal and simply asks what services they offer instead of making assumptions from unreliable web data.
Example Business: Tony Broughton (Handyman, Yanchep WA); Aaron Davy (Electrician, Kaleen ACT); Martin Ferguson (Fencer, VIC — personal name returns politician web results, AI correctly ignores them)
Example Detail: 959 sole traders in contacts CSV. Code: _is_sole_trader_personal_name() checks _has_registered_trading_name flag, entity type, and SURNAME regex. Data confidence in service discovery prompt: no licence + no Google + no category = LOW CONFIDENCE warning tells AI to ignore web results and just ask.

Edge Case: Pty Ltd where registered name differs from trading name (e.g. "J & K Holdings Pty Ltd" trades as "Pete's Plumbing")
Our Response: ABR enrichment fetches both trading name and entity name. Card shows trading name as label, legal name as subtitle when different. Licence searched under legal name; suffix stripped by match_licence().
Example Business: JAMGAV Pty Ltd T/A GERRY LOUIS ELECTRICAL; Accede Holdings Pty Ltd; R&T Building Co Pty Ltd T/A Mon Reve Maison
Example Detail: 19 Holdings/Investment Pty Ltds and 6 explicit T/A structures in contacts. If licence not found under company name, asks for personal name (_needs_licence_holder_name).

Edge Case: Trust structure (e.g. "Smith Family Trust") / Partnership
Our Response: If licence search under trust/company name returns 0 results, detects trust/holdings keywords via _ENTITY_PATTERNS regex and asks "What name is the licence under?" User provides personal name for fresh licence search.
Example Business: FLASH CONCRETING DISCRETIONARY TRUST (Concreter, Mackay QLD); L.D. Electrical Contractor Pty Ltd ATF Le Family Trust (Electrician, Noble Park VIC)
Example Detail: 73 trusts and 5 partnerships in contacts. Gap: partnerships not in _ENTITY_PATTERNS regex — if legal name is "SMITH AND JONES" the fallback prompt won't fire.

Edge Case: Cancelled or inactive ABN
Our Response: Two-layer filtering: (1) enrichment fetches real ABN status, inactive results filtered before card display. (2) At confirmation, status re-checked — if cancelled, blocks with "You'll need an active ABN to join Service Seeking."
Example Business: Any inactive ABN (filtered out so not visible)
Example Detail: Code: graph.py line 202 pre-filter + line 2406 confirmation guard. Name search assumes Active (no per-entry status); enrichment pass fetches real status.

Edge Case: Very common/ambiguous business name returning multiple ABR matches
Our Response: Show up to 8 cards with business name, postcode, entity type. Near-duplicate detection adds "Company" or "Sole Trader" labels when same name exists as both. 5+ results prompts "try adding your postcode". Postcode in input auto-filters results.
Example Business: Jim's Antennas (257 Jim's franchise matches); ABC Electric Services vs ABC Electric Pty Ltd; A1 Builders Pty Ltd
Example Detail: Cap of 8 results shown (overflow discarded). User must know ABN or postcode if their business is beyond position 8.

Edge Case: User gives minimal input (just a first name, or just a suburb)
Our Response: Min 2-char guard. Single first name like "John" returns ABR results (likely noise) — shown as cards, user can reject and retry. Suburb names return 0 results with "try a different name or enter your ABN" prompt. LLM interprets unrelated input as question intent and guides user back.
Example Business: N/A — handled by conversation flow
Example Detail: Code: graph.py line 159 length guard. LLM intent classifier handles ambiguous input gracefully.

Edge Case: Business is not a trade or professional service (e.g. auction house; restaurant; retail shop; real estate agency)
Our Response: LLM classifier (Haiku) reads Google Places data (name, type, reviews) + website text and determines if the business belongs on Service Seeking. Returns is_trade=false for non-trade businesses. Replaces brittle keyword blocklist (_NON_TRADE_TYPES). If blocked, user sees a clear message and can try a different business.
Example Business: Smith Broughton Industrial Auctioneers (auction house, Midland WA — Google type "service" would have passed old keyword gate); any restaurant or retail shop
Example Detail: classify_business_from_web() in graph.py. Single Haiku call after _confirm_business. Handles cases old keyword gate missed: auction houses typed as "service", equipment dealers, recruitment agencies. Defaults to is_trade=true if no web data (safe pass-through for ghost businesses).

Edge Case: NSW licensed trade (direct register lookup)
Our Response: NSW Fair Trading API (OAuth2). Browse by legal name, match_licence() scores on name overlap + trade relevance. Extracts licence classes, contact name from associatedParties.
Example Business: Rainman Plumbing Pty Ltd (Plumber, Sydney NSW); Greater Sydney Electrical (Electrician, Parramatta NSW)
Example Detail: Direct API integration. Apostrophe retry if 0 results. Suffix stripping for Pty Ltd matching.

Edge Case: QLD licensed trade (direct register lookup)
Our Response: QBCC CSV lookup by ABN + legal name. Same match_licence() scoring as NSW.
Example Business: FLASH CONCRETING DISCRETIONARY TRUST (Concreter, Mackay QLD); Concreting Qld Trading Trust
Example Detail: CSV-based lookup avoids API dependency.

Edge Case: Trade in VIC, SA, WA, TAS, NT, ACT (web search fallback for licence)
Our Response: _STATE_LICENCE_CONFIG defines regex patterns per state/trade. Brave + website text scanned for licence numbers. Falls back to self-report if no match found.
Example Business: PRO TILING TECH PTY LTD (Tiler, Craigieburn VIC); Aaron Mcgrath (Carpenter, Endeavour Hills VIC)
Example Detail: WA DMIRS old portal dead — all non-NSW/QLD states use web extraction + self-report. Config keyed by state → trade.

Edge Case: Trade that doesn't require licensing (e.g. fencer, landscaper, handyman)
Our Response: Licence lookup skipped entirely for non-licensed trades. Proceeds directly to service discovery without licence data.
Example Business: Tony Broughton (Handyman, Yanchep WA); Maxy Property Maintenance (Handyman, Caulfield VIC)
Example Detail: _STATE_LICENCE_CONFIG only has entries for licensed trades. No-licence trades flow straight through.

Edge Case: Expired or suspended licence
Our Response: match_licence() checks licence status. If expired/suspended, noted in licence_info but not blocked — business may still be renewing. LLM mentions it conversationally.
Example Business: N/A — depends on live licence register data
Example Detail: Licence status from API response passed through to state.

Edge Case: Full presence: website with services, logo, gallery, plus Google Business Profile
Our Response: Google Places searched first (website, reviews, photos, phone). Website scraped for logo + work photos. AI filter (Haiku vision) classifies images as WORK/SKIP. Reviews + website text fed into evidence keyword scanning for service mapping.
Example Business: Rainman Plumbing (rainmanplumbing.com.au, 708 reviews); Xtreme floor care (xtremefloorcare.com.au, 535 reviews)
Example Detail: Happy path: richest data for profile builder. Photos filtered by AI before display.

Edge Case: Social-only: no website, but active Instagram and/or Facebook with work photos
Our Response: When no website found, Brave searches site:instagram.com OR site:facebook.com. Filters by distinctive name words (excludes generic trade words). Presents candidates: "Is this yours?" User confirms before scraping.
Example Business: Berrima Bridge Horticulture (Facebook-only website in contacts)
Example Detail: Verified-only sourcing: never scrape unverified URLs. User must confirm social profile ownership.

Edge Case: Google Business Profile only: just a GMB listing with reviews
Our Response: Google Places Text Search returns reviews, rating, phone, suburb, photos even without a website. Reviews used for evidence scanning. Photos available for profile if user approves.
Example Business: Jim's Antennas Lynbrook (141 jobs, 50 reviews, no website)
Example Detail: Google Places is primary data source — website is supplementary.

Edge Case: Ghost: absolutely nothing online (new business or never set anything up)
Our Response: No website discovered, no Google Places match, no social profiles found. Profile builder generates description from ABN/licence data only. Monogram shown instead of logo. User can upload their own photos.
Example Business: KURMA VENKATA RAJ SHEKHAR (Photographer, no city, no website, 0 reviews); TOMEH PAINTING PTY LTD (Painter, 0 everything)
Example Detail: 187,028 ghost businesses in contacts (70% of dataset). Monogram fallback via getInitials().

Edge Case: Misleading results: common business name pulling wrong company's assets
Our Response: Google Places name matching requires 50% word overlap (not just 1 shared word) — prevents wrong-business data. Brave URL validation uses distinctive name words only. Brave query includes "Australia" to reduce US results. LLM classifier as final safety net: even if wrong Google data slips through, Haiku reads the content and correctly identifies trade vs non-trade.
Example Business: Broughton Construction Company → Google returned Smith Broughton Auctioneers (1/3 word overlap = discarded); ABC Electric Services vs ABC Electric Pty Ltd
Example Detail: Multi-layer: (1) Google name overlap ≥50%, (2) Brave distinctive words, (3) LLM classifier reads actual content. Old system: "electric winches" on auction site → falsely detected as Electrician.

Edge Case: Straightforward single-trade operator (e.g. domestic electrician)
Our Response: Single category detected → bulk mapping adds all subcategories from service_tiers.json. Only specialist ask items questioned. Fastest path through service discovery (often 2-3 turns).
Example Business: True Blue Electrical Australia (Electrician only, 208 jobs, 5.0 stars); XCEED Electrical Contractors
Example Detail: Cleanest onboarding path. Bulk mapping handles everything.

Edge Case: Multi-trade operator (e.g. builder who also does carpentry, tiling, project management)
Our Response: Multiple categories detected from licence/Google/name. Each category bulk-mapped independently. Related category suggestions offer co-occurrence-based additions. LLM clusters specialist gaps across all categories.
Example Business: Manno Plumbing (10 industries including Builder, Plumber, Gas Fitter, Gardener, Roofer); Maxy Property Maintenance (6 industries)
Example Detail: Stress test for service discovery. 10-industry operators generate many specialist gaps — adaptive fallback triggers multi-select UI earlier.

Edge Case: Adjacent trade scenario (e.g. plumber who also does gas fitting and roofing)
Our Response: Adjacent trades appear in related_categories.json co-occurrence data. After primary category mapping, system suggests related categories (e.g. Gas Fitter for Plumber). Accepted categories get bulk-mapped too.
Example Business: Rhino Plumbing (Plumber + Gas Fitter + Bathroom Reno, 853 jobs); AABAA Electrical (Electrician + Solar + Security + Lighting, 712 jobs)
Example Detail: Very common pattern. Related category suggestions powered by analyse_categories.py co-occurrence data from real SS businesses.

Edge Case: Deep specialist (e.g. not a general electrician but specifically solar/EV charging)
Our Response: Specialist ask items in service_tiers.json checked against evidence (reviews/website/Brave). If evidence found (e.g. "solar" in reviews), auto-mapped as source:evidence. If not, LLM asks specifically about specialist services.
Example Business: Pool Fence Solutions (Carpenter + Fencing niche, 5.0 stars); First and Last Reblocking (Builder but niche underpinning specialist)
Example Detail: Specialists often listed under broader Industry categories. Evidence scanning catches niche keywords from reviews/website.

Edge Case: Generalist with shallow coverage (e.g. handyman who does a bit of everything)
Our Response: Handyman tier in service_tiers.json covers broad range. Bulk mapping adds general handyman subcats. Specialist gaps asked via LLM clustering. Adaptive fallback prevents too many questions for broad-but-shallow operators.
Example Business: Maxy Property Maintenance (Handyman primary, 1131 jobs, 134 reviews); Tony Broughton (Handyman, Yanchep WA, 3 jobs)
Example Detail: Top handyman businesses actually list it alongside primary trade. Bulk mapping handles breadth; specialist Qs handle depth.

Edge Case: Hyperlocal: works in 3-4 suburbs only
Our Response: User specifies suburbs directly. System maps to regions from suburbs.csv. Small suburb list = Standard plan recommendation (≤2 regions).
Example Business: A.W.Swann Plumbing Fitzroy (Fitzroy SA); Aldinga Glass & Aluminium (Aldinga SA)
Example Detail: Hyperlocal businesses tend to be in regional/outer suburbs. Suburb grouping via geo helpers.

Edge Case: Regional zone: covers a defined region like Northern Beaches or Inner West
Our Response: User names the region. System maps to constituent suburbs from suburbs.csv regional data. Matched against *_regions.md guides for Sydney/Melbourne/Brisbane/Perth.
Example Business: All Sydney Trees and Garden (Castle Hill NSW, 3 jobs)
Example Detail: Regional guides in resources/ define suburb groupings. LLM understands region names from guide context.

Edge Case: Wide metro: works across all of Greater Sydney
Our Response: User says "all of Sydney" or similar. System maps to all Greater Sydney regions. Pro plan recommended (>5 regions).
Example Business: Rainman Plumbing (Sydney NSW, 809 jobs across metro)
Example Detail: Wide metro = many regions = higher plan recommendation.

Edge Case: Cross-boundary: lives in one area, works in a completely different region
Our Response: ABR postcode used as hint but not constraint. User specifies actual work area regardless of registered address. No validation against ABN location.
Example Business: 5 Star Insulation (City: Lalor, State: ACT, Postcode: 3075 VIC — state mismatch)
Example Detail: 293 state/postcode mismatches in contacts. System trusts user-specified work area over ABR data.

Edge Case: Interstate: ABN registered in one state, operates in another
Our Response: ABN state determines licence lookup state. If operating interstate, licence lookup may miss (different state register). Web extraction fallback handles cross-state operation. Service area set independently of ABN state.
Example Business: Akopia Services (City: Glenwood NSW, State: NT, 15 industries)
Example Detail: Licence lookup uses ABR state — may need to search multiple state registers for interstate operators. Currently only searches one state.

Edge Case: All data available — tests happy path renders cleanly
Our Response: Full profile card: hero with SS blue gradient, logo/photos from website, LLM-generated description + intro, licence info, categories, editable services + areas. Pencil toggle for editing.
Example Business: Rainman Plumbing (website, 708 reviews, multiple trades, Sydney)
Example Detail: Happy path: website photos filtered by AI, Google reviews for evidence, full licence data, rich description.

Edge Case: Minimal data — tests placeholder handling, description generation from sparse info
Our Response: Monogram instead of logo. LLM generates description from ABN data + trade category only. Camera hint on hover. Upload prompt for photos. Placeholder handling for missing fields.
Example Business: KURMA VENKATA RAJ SHEKHAR (no city, no website, 0 reviews, 0 jobs)
Example Detail: 70% of contacts are ghosts. Description generated from minimal signals. Upload endpoint available for user photos.

Edge Case: Mixed quality — some fields rich, others empty
Our Response: Some cards have data, others show placeholders. Description quality varies by available context. Website photos may exist but licence data missing, or vice versa.
Example Business: PRO TILING TECH PTY LTD (4 industries listed but 0 jobs, 0 reviews, no website, Craigieburn VIC)
Example Detail: Common scenario: business registered on SS with category data but never engaged. Profile builder works with whatever's available.
